heading.title: Sentry
heading.subtitle: Connect Sentry to capture, group, and alert on application errors in real time.

create.title: Connect Sentry
create.detailsHint: Give a descriptive name so you can identify which Sentry project this entry represents.
create.connectionHint: Enter the DSN from your Sentry project settings. Credentials are stored encrypted.

placeholder.name: e.g. Sentry Production
placeholder.keepDsn: Leave empty to keep saved DSN

hint.selfHostedOnly: This is a platform-wide integration, configurable only on self-hosted deployments. It's read-only here.
hint.dsnRequired: Find your DSN in Sentry under Settings → Projects → Client Keys.
hint.dsnProtected: Credential is encrypted and cannot be retrieved after saving.
hint.environmentOptional: Filter events by environment. Leave empty to capture all.
hint.deleteWarning: This will permanently remove the Sentry integration configuration.
hint.simulateError: Sends a test exception to Sentry to verify the integration is working.
hint.summary: Sentry captures unhandled exceptions and tool execution errors from this platform, groups them by stack trace, and sends alerts when error rates spike.
hint.findDsnPath: Find your DSN in Sentry under Settings → Projects → [your project] → Client Keys (DSN).
hint.findClientKeys: Find your DSN in Sentry under Settings → Projects → [your project] → Client Keys.
hint.revealHint: The DSN is stored encrypted and never returned in API responses. Use the Reveal button to load it for editing.

empty.noProviders: No Sentry integrations yet. Click Connect Sentry to add one.

confirm.deleteMessage: This Sentry integration configuration will be permanently removed.
confirm.disconnectMessage: This will remove the DSN and stop forwarding errors. You can reconnect at any time.

status.activeForwarding: Active — forwarding error events to Sentry

help.title: Sentry Error Tracking
help.summary: Sentry captures unhandled exceptions and tool execution errors from this platform, groups them by stack trace, and sends alerts when error rates spike.
help.findDsnPath: Find your DSN in Sentry under Settings → Projects → [your project] → Client Keys (DSN).
help.enterFields: Paste the DSN, then set an environment and project name if you want events separated and labelled in Sentry.
help.activateAndSave: Enable forwarding and save. Only the active integration receives new Arthur error events.
help.dsnEffect: DSN selects the Sentry organization and project that receives events and contains the credentials needed to send them.
help.environmentEffect: Environment labels events, such as production or staging, so Sentry filters and alerts can distinguish deployments. Blank defaults to production.
help.projectEffect: Project name is sent as the Sentry release value and helps group events from this Arthur deployment.
help.activeEffect: Active starts forwarding unhandled backend and Tool execution errors; inactive keeps the configuration but pauses forwarding.
help.verify: Click Test connection first, then Send Test Event. Confirm the event appears in the selected Sentry project with the expected environment before relying on alerts.
help.findClientKeys: Find your DSN in Sentry under Settings → Projects → [your project] → Client Keys.
help.revealHint: The DSN is stored encrypted and omitted from normal API responses; reveal it only when an authorised editor must rotate it. Error events can include request paths, user identifiers, stack traces, and execution context, so review Sentry access and retention policies before enabling production forwarding. Sensitive query parameters are redacted, but secrets should never be placed in paths or error messages.
